from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

import httpx


class APIError(Exception):
    """Raised when the remote API returns a 4xx or 5xx status."""
    def __init__(self, status_code: int, body: Optional[str] = None):
        super().__init__(f'API error {status_code}: {body}')
        self.status_code = status_code
        self.body = body


class APIClient:
    """Async API client. Wraps httpx.AsyncClient."""

    def __init__(self, base_url: str, timeout: float = 5.0):
        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip('/')
        self.timeout = timeout

    async def fetch_user(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """GET /users/{user_id} → JSON dict. Raises APIError on 4xx/5xx,
        re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/users/{user_id}')
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def create_post(self, author_id: int, title: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """POST /posts. Body must be {author_id, title}. Returns the created post."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            r = await client.post(
                f'{self.base_url}/posts',
                json={'author_id': author_id, 'title': title},
            )
            if r.status_code >= 400:
                raise APIError(r.status_code, r.text)
            return r.json()

    async def ping(self) -> bool:
        """GET /healthz → True iff response is 200."""
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            try:
                r = await client.get(f'{self.base_url}/healthz')
                return r.status_code == 200
            except httpx.TransportError:
                return False
post_state_files.test_client.py:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch, MagicMock
import httpx

from client import APIClient, APIError


@pytest.fixture
def client():
    return APIClient(base_url="http://test.example.com")


class TestFetchUser:
    """Tests for fetch_user method."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_fetch_user_success(self, client):
        """Test successful user fetch returns JSON dict."""
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 200
        mock_response.json.return_value = {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe"}

        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.get = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            result = await client.fetch_user(1)

            assert result == {"id": 1, "name": "John Doe"}
            mock_client.get.assert_called_once_with(
                "http://test.example.com/users/1"
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_fetch_user_4xx_error(self, client):
        """Test fetch_user raises APIError on 4xx response."""
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 404
        mock_response.text = "User not found"

        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.get = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
                await client.fetch_user(999)

            assert exc_info.value.status_code == 404
            assert exc_info.value.body == "User not found"

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_fetch_user_network_error(self, client):
        """Test fetch_user re-raises httpx.TransportError on network failure."""
        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.get = AsyncMock(
                side_effect=httpx.TransportError("Connection failed")
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            with pytest.raises(httpx.TransportError):
                await client.fetch_user(1)


class TestCreatePost:
    """Tests for create_post method."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_create_post_success(self, client):
        """Test successful post creation returns JSON dict."""
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 201
        mock_response.json.return_value = {
            "id": 42,
            "author_id": 1,
            "title": "Test Post"
        }

        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.post = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            result = await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="Test Post")

            assert result == {"id": 42, "author_id": 1, "title": "Test Post"}
            mock_client.post.assert_called_once_with(
                "http://test.example.com/posts",
                json={"author_id": 1, "title": "Test Post"}
            )

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_create_post_4xx_error(self, client):
        """Test create_post raises APIError on 4xx response."""
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 400
        mock_response.text = "Invalid title"

        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.post = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            with pytest.raises(APIError) as exc_info:
                await client.create_post(author_id=1, title="")

            assert exc_info.value.status_code == 400
            assert exc_info.value.body == "Invalid title"


class TestPing:
    """Tests for ping method."""

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ping_success(self, client):
        """Test ping returns True on 200 response."""
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 200

        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.get = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            result = await client.ping()

            assert result is True

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ping_non_200(self, client):
        """Test ping returns False on non-200 response."""
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.status_code = 503

        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.get = AsyncMock(return_value=mock_response)
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            result = await client.ping()

            assert result is False

    @pytest.mark.asyncio
    async def test_ping_network_error(self, client):
        """Test ping returns False on network error."""
        with patch('httpx.AsyncClient') as mock_client_class:
            mock_client = AsyncMock()
            mock_client.get = AsyncMock(
                side_effect=httpx.TransportError("Connection refused")
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aenter__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=mock_client
            )
            mock_client_class.return_value.__aexit__ = AsyncMock(
                return_value=None
            )

            result = await client.ping()

            assert result is False
